chapter: ENG9_6 – Weathering the Storm in Ersama
title: Weathering the Storm in Ersama – Medium VSAQs

questions.question: Why had Prashant gone to Ersama?
questions.answer: To spend the day with a friend.

questions.question: What made the night of the cyclone terrifying?
questions.answer: Strong winds, heavy rain, and rising floodwaters.

questions.question: Why did the family climb onto the roof?
questions.answer: To escape the rising floodwaters inside the house.

questions.question: Why was the falling of coconut trees called a blessing in disguise?
questions.answer: Because the coconuts helped them survive without food.

questions.question: What was Prashant constantly thinking about during the storm?
questions.answer: Whether his family had survived the cyclone.

questions.question: Why did Prashant decide to return to his village?
questions.answer: To find out if his family was safe.

questions.question: Why was Prashant’s journey difficult?
questions.answer: Because the roads were flooded and full of debris.

questions.question: What shocking sights did Prashant see on his way?
questions.answer: Dead bodies of people and animals floating in the water.

questions.question: What did Prashant feel when he reached his village?
questions.answer: Fear and sadness seeing the destruction.

questions.question: Why had villagers gathered at the Red Cross shelter?
questions.answer: Because their homes had been destroyed.

questions.question: How many lives were lost in Kalikuda village?
questions.answer: Eighty-six lives.

questions.question: What food did the villagers initially survive on?
questions.answer: Green coconuts.

questions.question: Why did Prashant organise a group of volunteers?
questions.answer: To help manage relief and support the villagers.

questions.question: How did the villagers finally get rice?
questions.answer: By pressuring the merchant through a group effort.

questions.question: Why was cleaning the shelter important?
questions.answer: To prevent disease and improve living conditions.

questions.question: What message did the children send to helicopters?
questions.answer: That they were hungry and needed food.

questions.question: Why were many children orphaned?
questions.answer: Because their parents died in the cyclone.

questions.question: How did Prashant help the orphaned children?
questions.answer: By arranging shelter and care for them.

questions.question: Why did Prashant organise sports for children?
questions.answer: To help them forget their grief.

questions.question: What programme helped women rebuild their lives?
questions.answer: The food-for-work programme.

questions.question: Why did Prashant resist sending orphans to institutions?
questions.answer: Because children needed family love and support.

questions.question: What role did the women play after the cyclone?
questions.answer: They cared for orphaned children.

questions.question: What personal pain did Prashant ignore?
questions.answer: His own grief and suffering.

questions.question: Why did villagers respect Prashant?
questions.answer: Because of his leadership and courage.

questions.question: What quality helped Prashant lead the community?
questions.answer: Determination and compassion.
